title: Haplostachys linearifolia
what_description: Haplostachys linearifolia is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Lamiaceae. It is a shrub that grows up to 1 meter in height, with linear leaves that are 2-5 centimeters long and 0.5-1.5 millimeters wide, and are oppositely arranged on the stem. The leaves are glabrous, with a cuneate base and a linear apex, and are not lobed or toothed. The inflorescence is a terminal spike of 2-4 millimeter long flowers that are purple or white in color, and are zygomorphic and bilabiate. The flowers have a calyx with 5 lobes, and a corolla with 4 lobes, and are fragrant.

taxonomy: Haplostachys linearifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is a rare and endemic plant found in the Society Islands of French Polynesia. This plant is classified as an evergreen shrub, growing up to a certain height and width. Haplostachys linearifolia is also known by its common name, linear-leaf Haplostachys. The classification of this plant places it within the larger family of dogbane and oleander plants.

intro: Haplostachys linearifolia is a species of flowering plant in the family Asclepiadaceae, commonly known as the "Linear-leaved Haplostachys". This plant has been traditionally used in Polynesian medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation and respiratory issues, due to its potential health benefits in reducing oxidative stress and improving immune function. The bioactive constituents of Haplostachys linearifolia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas and infusions, as well as extracts used in traditional medicine for their therapeutic applications.
